mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production above its 12-month moving average confirms robust manufacturing and utility output, which supports earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during late-cycle expansions, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96) when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations following GDPC1 releases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with a classification accuracy above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a panel regression over a 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production following a contraction phase signals rapid capacity utilization recovery, which accelerates corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >60% of post-contraction rebounds, or the signal fails to produce a positive alpha (p > 0.05) in a walk-forward test.
